originalTitle: Italian PM condemns ally Trump over 'unacceptable' Pope criticism
title: Friends, Popes, and Being Kind!
content:
A Little Chat About Big Leaders!

Hello, my super friends! It’s your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa here, ready to share a story from far, far away. Today, we are going to talk about some very important leaders from different countries. We have the leader of Italy, who is called the Prime Minister. And we have the leader of the United States of America, who is called the President. There is also a very special person called the Pope. These leaders are usually good friends, but sometimes friends can have disagreements. This story is about a time when the President said something that made the Prime Minister a little bit cross, all because of something he said about the Pope.

Who is the Pope? A Very Special Friend!

First, let’s talk about the Pope. The Pope is like a super important teacher for millions and millions of people all over the world who believe in God in a special way. He is the main leader of the Catholic Church. Our story is about Pope Leo XIV. Popes are very old and wise. They live in a tiny, tiny country called Vatican City, which is inside another big country called Italy. It’s the smallest country in the whole wide world! The Pope’s job is to teach people about kindness, peace, and love. He tries to help everyone be good to each other. He often talks about helping people who need help and making the world a better place. Many, many people love and respect the Pope very much. They listen to his words and try to follow his advice. He is seen as a very spiritual and good person. Did you know? Vatican City is so small, you can walk across it in less than an hour! It even has its own post office!

Meet the President and the Prime Minister!

Now, let’s meet our other leaders! We have the President of the United States of America. The United States is a very big country, far away across the ocean. The President is like the head teacher of that whole country! He makes big decisions and helps lead the people. Then we have the Prime Minister of Italy. Italy is a beautiful country in Europe. It’s shaped like a boot on the map! The Prime Minister is the head leader of Italy, just like the President is the head leader of the USA. These two leaders, the President and the Prime Minister, are usually good friends. They like to work together to help their countries and other countries around the world. Did you know? The USA has 50 different states, like 50 small countries all joined together! Italy is famous for delicious food like pizza and pasta!

Friends and Allies: Why They Work Together

When countries are "allies," it means they are like best friends! They help each other out. If one country has a problem, the other country tries to help. The USA and Italy have been friends for a very long time. They share ideas, trade things, and sometimes even work together to keep the world safe. This friendship, or being allies, is very important because it makes both countries stronger. When friends work together, they can do amazing things! So, when the leaders of allied countries talk, they usually try to be very polite and respectful to each other. They understand that even though they are friends, they are also very important leaders of their own people.

What Happened? Not-So-Kind Words

Okay, so here’s where the story gets a little tricky. The President of the USA said something about Pope Leo XIV. He said the Pope was "very weak." Oh dear! Those are not very kind words to say about anyone, especially not about someone as respected as the Pope. When someone says something like that, it’s called "criticism." It means they are saying bad things or complaining about someone. And calling someone "weak" is not a compliment, is it? It makes it sound like they are not strong or good at their job. This was a surprise to many people, especially because the Pope is seen as a very strong leader of faith and peace. Did you know? Sometimes people wear special white robes to show they are spiritual leaders, like the Pope!

The Italian Prime Minister is Upset!

When the President said these words, the Prime Minister of Italy heard them. And guess what? He was not happy! He thought what the President said was "unacceptable." That means he thought it was very, very wrong and not okay at all. The Prime Minister felt very strongly that you should always be respectful, especially to the Pope, who is such an important figure to many Italian people and Catholics worldwide. He said he "condemns" what the President said. When you "condemn" something, it means you are saying very loudly and clearly that you think it is terrible and wrong. It’s like saying, "No! This is not right!" The Prime Minister felt it was his job to stand up for the Pope and for respect. He probably thought, "Even though we are allies and friends, you still need to be kind and respectful to others!"

Saying Sorry: Why It's Important

After the President said those words, many people thought, "Oh, maybe he will say sorry?" When you say something unkind, the best thing to do is to "apologize." To apologize means to say, "I am sorry for what I said" or "I am sorry for what I did." It helps to make things better and fix friendships. It shows that you understand you made a mistake and you want to be kind again. But in this story, the US President said he would "refuse to apologize." That means he would not say sorry to Pope Leo XIV. This made the situation even more difficult, because usually, when friends have a disagreement, saying sorry helps everyone feel better and move forward. When someone won't apologize, it can make it harder for the other person to forgive them.

Let's Learn About Respect!

So, what can we learn from this story? Even big, important leaders sometimes have disagreements. But the most important lesson is about respect. It means treating everyone with kindness, even if you don't always agree with them. The Pope is a very important spiritual leader, and many people look up to him. Saying unkind things about him can hurt many people's feelings. It’s always good to think before we speak. Ask yourself: "Is this kind? Is this helpful? Is this respectful?" If the answer is no, maybe it's better not to say it. Being kind and respectful helps us to be good friends and good neighbours in our homes, our schools, and even between countries. And when we do make a mistake, saying sorry is a brave and kind thing to do. Let's all try to be respectful and kind, just like the Pope teaches us!
summary: The leader of Italy is upset because the President of the United States said unkind things about Pope Leo XIV and won't say sorry.
whyMatters: This story reminds us that even important leaders should be kind and respectful to everyone, and saying sorry helps mend friendships when mistakes are made.
